At Masters Linea, we design, produce, and launch premium clothing and fashion lines, done for you. From first sketch to finished garments. You bring the vision. We bring the expertise.

The Studio
By Referral Only · Est. 2024

Masters Linea is the invisible hand behind the label. We work privately with founders and creators, taking a brand from first concept through to a finished, market-ready product — while you remain the face of it. Design, production, campaign, and launch, handled end to end.

Services

Two phases.
One complete brand.

Work with us for Phase 1 only, or take your brand all the way to market with Phase 2.

01

Phase One

Brand Creation

Design · Technical Documents · Factory Production · Sampling

 

We take your vision and turn it into a production-ready clothing line. Every garment is designed with intention, documented to the highest standard, and sourced and manufactured through our partners.

  • One-to-one design consultation
  • Collection design and creative direction
  • Technical drawings and flat sketches
  • Full technical document creation
  • Sample development
02

Phase Two

Brand to Market

Campaign & Marketing · E-Commerce · Logistics & Retail Operations

We build the commercial infrastructure around your brand. Campaign direction, marketing strategy, a fully operational e-commerce store, and the retail systems to sustain long-term growth.

  • Campaign direction and consultation
  • Brand marketing strategy
  • E-commerce store build and management
  • Paid and organic channel strategy
  • Inventory, fulfilment and retail operations

Our Process.

01

Design

Creative consultation and direction. Collection design. Technical drawings. Full technical documents — every garment documented to industry standard.

02

Production

Sample production and manufacturing. We manage the entire production relationship from brief to finished garment.

03

Campaign

Shoot direction, content production, and brand storytelling. We build the visual identity that sells the product.

04

Market

eCom store, marketing strategy, fulfilment, and retail operations. Built to scale from the first drop.

Masters Linea

The Studio · Events

The
Mastermind.

Masters Linea is the studio behind the label — and the room where it's taught. Private evenings, masterminds, and the frameworks behind building a clothing brand from zero. Hosted, shot, and staged by the studio.

A Private Evening · Canggu

How to build a
million-dollar clothing brand.

Fashion isn't taught the way it's actually run. Design schools teach the creative lens — almost nobody teaches the business: margins, sourcing, sell-through, scaling. That gap is where most brands die, and where the real money is made.

The premise: twelve months, a standing start, one million in revenue. Here's exactly what to do.

Full talk — coming to YouTube

The Knowledge

The frameworks
behind the brand.

A window into how the studio thinks. The principles we build on — the same ones we apply to every brand we take from concept to market.

01

Two kinds of brand

Every label sits on a spectrum. Commercial solves a need — "I need a white top for Friday" — and competes on price and convenience. Stylized solves an identity — "I want to be seen a certain way" — and never competes on price. A Casio tells the time better than a Rolex. Nobody buys a Rolex for the time.

02

The Rule of Three

How many times you can re-run a winning style before it dies. Once — the buyers who missed it are waiting. Twice — maybe. Three times — never. The odds get worse every run, because humans crave new. The move is a new style adjacent to the winner. Variations reset the clock.

03

The Revenue Engine

The business is an engine; revenue is its fuel. It only runs if you keep feeding it — every pound back into stock, content, visibility. Take money out for a watch or a holiday and you're siphoning your own fuel. Five sales become ten, then twenty, forty, eighty. Discipline is the whole game.

04

The Five Tells

The signs a brand is dying, spottable in ninety seconds. The luxury lie — claims luxury, executes cheap. The discount tell — half the range on sale. No price logic. No cohesion. And the invisible product — if they can't see it, they can't want it. Once you see them, you can't unsee them.

05

Don't discount

Price signals perceived value. A discount tells the market the product failed and burns your margin at the same time. Overvalue almost always — sell sixty to seventy percent, cover every cost, keep the profit. A "no" at one price is often a "yes" at a higher one.

06

The brand is the product

Long-term, people don't come for a garment — they come for you. Half of why they buy is the style; half is the experience they trust, because they've bought before. A collection with no narrative is just inventory. Cohesion is what makes pieces a family.
